content.intro: Personal Injury Accident Attorney in Ashland, Nebraska.

sections.content: Personal Injury Accident Attorney in Ashland, Nebraska. The Bull Attorneys helps injury victims and is an accident attorney practicing across the State of Nebraska including Ashland and Saunders County. Bull Attorneys has personal injury attorneys and personal injury lawyers who are truly experienced in getting money for people involved in personal injury accidents. Our personal injury lawyers handle auto accidents, car accidents, motorcycle accidents and our personal injury attorneys handle truck accidents, tractor-trailer accidents and semi accidents.

Ashland benefits by its proximity to Interstate 80 and the cities of Omaha and Lincoln. While in some respects Ashland is becoming a "bedroom community" of those much larger cities, it retains a rural character. That coherence as a community, and a 30-minute drive to either of the state's economic hubs, has led to a building boom in the late 1990s and 2000s. Employers in the Ashland area include Innovative Laboratory Systems, Linoma Software, Camp Ashland (Nebraska Army National Guard Training Center), and Trade Well Pallet. Ashland has many interesting and unique people making it a cultural and interesting place to visit.
People needing an accident attorney or lawyer in the Ashland and Saunders County area can get an accident attorney quickly by looking at for a free consultation.

The ZIP code for Ashland is 68003. Ashland has a 2013 population of approximately 2,500 people. Ashland and Saunders County are known for having very dangerous highway areas for traffic accidents that are fatal. Ashland can be reached from a group of highways including Interstate 80 and Highway 6 and other roads. Ashland's highways have substantial traffic flow of tractor-trailers, big rigs and 18-wheelers. The shoulders on these roads are very narrow. A large number of Nebraskans are killed or severely injured every year due to the negligence of large trucks driving across the state on these dangerous highways to avoid toll and weigh stations. Be careful when traveling on including Nebraska roads, including Interstate 80 and Highway 6, so that you and your family are safe.
